title: Login codes still going to old phone
goal: Route the ticket to the correct queue, set urgency, choose the correct response template, and submit only when the workspace is fully correct.
policy_notes: If the customer is locked out after a device or phone change, route to account_support.
Use the password_reset template for login recovery and MFA reset issues.
Do not escalate unless the ticket mentions unauthorized access, suspicious purchases, or account takeover.
Do not mark requires_refund unless the customer explicitly needs a refund or was charged incorrectly.

ticket.subject: Can't log in after switching phones
ticket.body: I replaced my phone yesterday and now the login code keeps going to the old number. I need to get back into my account before tomorrow morning. I do not see any suspicious purchases, I just cannot get in.

title: Charged twice and still no delivery
goal: Follow ownership precedence carefully. The ticket mentions both shipping and billing, but only one queue should own the case.
policy_notes: If a ticket includes a duplicate charge or direct refund request, billing_ops owns the case even if shipping is also mentioned.
Mark requires_refund true when the customer clearly asks for a refund or reports duplicate payment.
Use refund_review when money movement or duplicate charges are involved.
Escalation is false unless there is a security issue, legal threat, or safety concern.

ticket.subject: Order charged twice and package hasn't moved
ticket.body: Order 55192 has not arrived and the tracking link has been stuck for five days. On top of that, my card was charged twice for the same order. I either need the extra charge reversed or a full refund because this is taking too long.

title: Unauthorized purchase and account takeover signs
goal: Resolve conflicting signals correctly. The customer mentions a chargeback and refund, but the security incident still takes priority.
policy_notes: Any ticket with unauthorized purchases, changed shipping information, or account takeover signals must route to trust_safety.
Security incidents are urgent and require escalation.
Use security_hold when the account must be secured before any billing outcome is processed.
Do not set requires_refund during initial triage when fraud investigation and account security take precedence.

ticket.subject: Someone bought something on my account and changed my details
ticket.body: I woke up to a purchase I did not make and then got kicked out of my account. The shipping address was changed and I have already emailed twice without hearing back. If this is not handled tonight I am filing a chargeback and posting screenshots everywhere.
